newsletters.title: From Doing to Tending
newsletters.content:
Plus: A mini-Vibe Check of Grok 4.5, and why AI scribes could dull doctors' judgment  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjB9

Context Window

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY29udGV4dC13aW5kb3ciLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MX0=


FROM DOING TO TENDING https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY29udGV4dC13aW5kb3cvZnJvbS1kb2luZy10by10ZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjJ9


PLUS: A MINI-VIBE CHECK OF GROK 4.5, AND WHY AI SCRIBES COULD DULL DOCTORS' JUDGMENT https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY29udGV4dC13aW5kb3cvZnJvbS1kb2luZy10by10ZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjN9

by Every Staff https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQEV2ZXJ5JTIwU3RhZmYiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NH0=

Midjourney/Every illustration.

Was this newsletter forwarded to you? Sign up https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vYWNjb3VudCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo1fQ== to get it in your inbox.



----------------------------------------




MINI-VIBE CHECK: GROK 4.5 IS FAST, CHEAP, AND FINALLY USEFUL

Almost a year ago, we vibe-checked Grok 4 https://every. and found a model that performed well on benchmarks but was not useful enough for our engineers to use every day. Our April Vibe Check of Cursor 3.0 https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vdmliZS1jaGVjay9jdXJzb3IiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6N30= found a promising but unfinished agent-orchestration product, but that verdict aged quickly: Kieran Klaassen https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQGtpZXJhbl8xMzU1IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjh9 was using Cursor daily by the end of the month, and by June Composer 2.5 was his main model for final polish. Then, in June, SpaceX signed an agreement to acquire Cursor https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vd3d3LmNuYmMuY29tLzIwMjYvMDYvMTYvc3BhY2V4LXNwY3gtY3Vyc29yLWFjcXVpc2l0aW9uLWlwby5odG1sIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjl9, and a new candidate for the AI frontier space race was born.

Grok 4.5 https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8veC5haS9uZXdzL2dyb2stNC01IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEwfQ== is the first result of that collaboration. https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8veC5haS9uZXdzL2dyb2stNC01IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjExfQ== Cursor says it jointly trained the model https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vY3Vyc29yLmNvbS9ibG9nL2dyb2stNC01IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEyfQ== with SpaceXAI using data from interactions with codebases and software tools.

When our team ran Grok 4.5 through the evals we use internally, the consensus was that it is an Opus-level model. Mike Taylor https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQG1pa2VfMjExNCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxM30=’s latest benchmark put it slightly above Claude Opus 4.8 https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vdmliZS1jaGVjay9vcHVzLTQtOC12aWJlY2hlY2siLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTR9: Grok followed every step and returned a complete, polished result, while Opus stopped early or skipped parts of the assignment.

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8veC5jb20vaGFtbWVyX210L3N0YXR1cy8yMDc0OTQxODY5MDYzMDMzMTk2IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjE1fQ==Mike shared his early impressions of Grok-4.5 on X, including what he deems Opus-level PowerPoint creation. (Image courtesy of X/Mike Taylor.)




Kieran ran Grok 4.5 through /LFG, Every’s compound-engineering https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vZ3VpZGVzL2NvbXBvdW5kLWVuZ2luZWVyaW5nIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjE2fQ== workflow for planning, building, reviewing, and improving a project. He put it around Claude’s Opus 4.5 https://every.-to-4.6 https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vdmliZS1jaGVjay9vcHVzLTQtNiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxOH0= range, calling it “not state of the art, but pretty good for a lot of things, and very fast.”

Grok’s biggest competitive advantage may be its impact on the bottom line: xAI says Grok 4.5 runs at roughly 80 tokens per second and about twice the token efficiency of leading models. At $2 per million input tokens and $6 per million output, it is also much cheaper than the frontier models Every compared it with: Claude Opus 4.8 costs $5 and $25, while GPT-5.6 Sol htt

newsletters.title: How GPT-5.6 Changes Knowledge Work
newsletters.content:
Don’t do your work. Tend your loop.  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvLyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjowfQ==

Chain of Thought

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NoYWluLW9mLXRob3VnaHQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MX0=


HOW GPT-5.6 CHANGES KNOWLEDGE WORK https://every.


DON’T DO YOUR WORK. TEND YOUR LOOP. https://every.

by Dan Shipper https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BkYW5zaGlwcGVyIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjR9

Midjourney/Every illustration.

TL;DR: GPT-5.6 is the first model I’ve used that can reliably run whole loops of knowledge work, not just help with individual tasks. Your job turns from doing the work to tending the system that does it. If you want to try this for yourself, we’re open-sourcing a prompt and repository called Tend https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL3RlbmQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NX0= so you can try working this way in ChatGPT Work.

Experiment with Tend https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL3RlbmQ_c291cmNlPXBvc3RfYnV0dG9uIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjZ9



----------------------------------------



GPT-5.6 heralds a new way of doing knowledge work.

Instead of using AI to complete one task at a time, you build a system that scans the available information, turns it into proposed decisions, and carries out the ones you approve. Over time, the system compounds your feedback to do more and more on its own.

This changes your job. It requires you to see your work as—dare I say it—a loop https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NvbnRleHQtd2luZG93L2xvb3BzLWZvci1ub24tY29kZXJzIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjd9. You go from doing all of the work yourself to tending the system that does it for you:

https://every. knowledge work loop, with human judgment at the center. (Image courtesy of Dan Shipper/Claude/GPT Image.)




Take email. I used to go through my inbox like this: Email comes in, I read it, I reply, I archive. (Or, email comes in, I open it, I close it, I wait several weeks, I open it again, I archive it.)

With GPT-5.6 Sol https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL3ZpYmUtY2hlY2svZ3B0LTUtNiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo5fQ== in the new ChatGPT Work app, formerly known as Codex, the process looks very different. GPT-5.6 Sol watches my inbox, decides what deserves my attention, does any necessary research, and presents each email with a concise summary and proposed reply. I either approve the draft or use Monologue https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3d3dy5tb25vbG9ndWUudG8vIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEwfQ== to dictate what I want changed. Then I move to the next email.

At the end of each sweep through my inbox, the agent derives my preferences from my revisions and decisions and remembers them for next time.

It’s no coincidence this sounds like compound engineering https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2d1aWRlcy9jb21wb3VuZC1lbmdpbmVlcmluZyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMX0=; it’s the same philosophy—just applied to knowledge work. I’ve been writing about this shift for a few years: In early 2024 I argued that much knowledge work would become managing agents https://every., and later that it would look like tending to a garden https://every.—creating the conditions for work to happen, rather than doing everything yourself directly. This isn’t a new way of working: Managers and entrepreneurs have done it for decades, and as models improved over the past year, programmers adopted it too. Now it’s knowledge work’s turn.

This approach won’t work for every kind of knowledge work, and it’s still early. But where it works, it creates a remarkable kind of leverage.


WHAT MAKES GPT-5.6 SOL AND CHATGPT WORK DIFFERENT

GPT-5.6 Sol crosses the threshold that makes a continuous knowledge-work loop practical. It can scan your sources, identify what’s relevant, carry out approved work, and build custom tools for itself as needed. It can do all of this reliably even if you can’t code—and it can explain all of this to you in a way that’s understandable.

It’s also fast and cheap enough that you can iterate rapidly—a crucial requirement for non-technical users who are going to make mistakes and need to see the results of a run before knowing i

The Voices Dataset Catalogue gives AI and ML teams immediate access to professionally directed Voice Data: character performances spanning 450+ unique types, expressive speech across 43 emotional states, and multilingual recordings across 8 languages (part of 100,000+ hours of premium Voice Data available from Voices).

Every dataset is fully consented, production-ready, and structured for immediate pipeline integration.

Three specialized datasets. Real range. Zero sourcing headaches.

#### **THROUGH THE VALLEY**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/819080f2-3abe-4b50-9e42-55b62ec48eb0/image_-_2026-07-10T200328.714__1_.png?t=1783694422)
Caption: Courtesy: Anthropic

**1/ OpenAI’s new “Super App” wants to be your co-worker: **The company launched [ChatGPT Work](https://openai.com/index/chatgpt-for-your-most-ambitious-work/), a GPT-5.6-powered workspace that combines chat, coding, automation, and workplace integrations to complete complex tasks with minimal supervision. Connected to tools like Slack, Microsoft Teams, Google Drive, SharePoint, and Salesforce, it can work for hours on multi-step projects, bringing OpenAI into direct competition with Anthropic's Claude Cowork and Microsoft Copilot. ([watch the announcement live stream here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Wq45rvPGNHs)_)_.

**2/ OpenAI just made ChatGPT conversations feel more human:** The company launched [GPT-Live](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-live), a new full-duplex voice model that allows ChatGPT to listen and speak simultaneously, enabling natural interruptions, faster responses, and more fluid back-and-forth conversations. The new experience also supports real-time interruptions, memory, search, and GPT-5.5-powered responses, bringing voice interactions closer to speaking with another person. ([watch the full livestream here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9f-Ew_lDtxc))

**3/ GPT-5.6 rolls out broadly as OpenAI’s new top AI model:** The company [launched](https://openai.com/index/gpt-5-6) GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna, a new family of models built for coding, reasoning, cybersecurity, and enterprise workloads. OpenAI says its flagship Sol outperforms Anthropic's Fable 5 while running faster, using 54% fewer tokens on agentic coding tasks, and costing roughly one-third less. GPT-5.6 Sol has been [ranked 2nd](https://x.com/ArtificialAnlys/status/2075268970492657905) to Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index. ([watch the launch here](https://x.com/OpenAI/status/2075271421149020426?s=20))

**4/ Anthropic is turning AI usage into something you can measure: **The company launched a new [reflection dashboard](https://x.com/claudeai/status/2075225957711929667?s=20) for Claude that analyzes conversations across recurring topics, task types, and usage patterns over the past year. Alongside privacy-focused insights, the feature adds quiet hours, break reminders, and self-reflection prompts to help users build healthier AI habits.

**5/ 1X just unveiled one of the most advanced humanoid robot hands yet: **The company's new [NEO hand](https://x.com/1x_tech/status/2075252899442204952?s=20) features 25 degrees of freedom, force sensing in every joint, tactile skin, and a lightweight tendon-driven design that closely mimics human dexterity. Built to be waterproof, durable, and mass-manufacturable, 1X says it already has the capacity to produce up to 10,000 hands this year.

**6/ SpaceXAI and Cursor just launched Grok's biggest upgrade yet:** The companies unveiled [Grok 4.5](https://x.ai/news/grok-4-5), a new flagship model that delivers Opus-class performance across coding, agentic, and knowledge tasks while running significantly faster and at a fraction of the cost. With benchmark results approaching Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5, Grok 4.5 positions itself as one of the strongest value-focused frontier AI models available.

**7/ Muse Spark 1.1 debuts as Meta’s advanced coding model:** The company launched [Muse Spark 1.1](https://x.com/alexandr_wang/status/2075218936266998230?s=20) alongside the public preview of the Meta Model API, bringing a multimodal reasoning model with a 1 million-token context window, advanced agent workflows, and stronger coding capabilities. Meta says the model delivers major improvements over its predecessor while enabling developers to build AI agents that can use tools, browse apps, and complete complex tasks autonomously.


----------

----------
# TRENDING TOOLS


--------------------
* [Reve 2.1](https://x.com/reve/status/2075248950756716747?s=20) > The world's leading 4K image generation model just got even better

* [GPT-Live](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-live) > OpenAI's new full duplex voice experience for ChatGPT enables natural, real time conversations with minimal latency

* [Notion Ship OS](https://x.com/NotionHQ/status/2075249002208227743) > Notion introduced Ship OS, an agent native workspace designed to help teams plan, build, and ship software faster

* [Ora Directory](https://directory.ora.ai/) > A searchable directory of tools, APIs, and resources that AI agents can discover and use

* [ChatGPT Work](https://openai.com/chatgpt-work) > A GPT-5.6 powered work agent that can plan tasks, browse the web, use connectors, generate deliverables, schedule work, and seamlessly continue across desktop and mobile

* [Muse Spark 1](https://ai.meta.com/blog/introducing-muse-spark-meta-model-api) > Meta unveiled Spark 1, its latest multimodal reasoning model built for advanced AI understanding and problem solving

* [Claude Cowork](https://x.com/claudeai/status/20745

newsletters.content:
Sol is fast, resourceful, and unusually easy to steer—but Fable still gets the assignments we want to hand off completely  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌

Vibe Check

https://every.


VIBE CHECK: GPT-5.6 SOL IS OUR FAVORITE MODEL TO COLLABORATE WITH https://every.


SOL IS FAST, RESOURCEFUL, AND UNUSUALLY EASY TO STEER—BUT FABLE STILL GETS THE ASSIGNMENTS WE WANT TO HAND OFF COMPLETELY https://every.

by Katie Parrott https://every.

Midjourney/Every illustration.

This is a free preview of a subscribers-only post.

Was this newsletter forwarded to you? Sign up to get it in your inbox.



----------------------------------------



At the end of June, we lost access to GPT-5.6 Sol while it went through government review. Dan Shipper said returning to older models felt like going back to the Stone Age. Austin Tedesco compared GPT-5.5 to shooting a basketball twice as heavy.

Now Sol is back. It’s become our default for work we want to shape as we go: writing, coding, research, and analysis inside OpenAI’s new unified ChatGPT and Codex desktop app. Sol is fast, unusually good at finding and using context, and easy to redirect. Anthropic’s Fable still leads on broad assignments that require the model to decide what should be built in the first place.

We get into all of it in our Vibe Check, including:

 * Why Sol scored 56/100 against Fable’s 90/100 on our Senior Engineer benchmark—and how 12,900 lines of unnecessary code explain most of the gap
 * How Kieran Klaassen rebuilt Proof with Sol in about one-third the time Fable needed, only to prefer Fable’s design
 * Why Sol finished last in our six-model writing benchmark while I (Katie Parrott) still used it to move through 24 drafts of this article in six to eight hours
 * How Sol found Arielle Shipper’s email, inspected 46 CSV files, and returned with seven useful questions—then made a calculation error serious enough to shake her trust


THIS POST IS FOR
PAYING SUBSCRIBERS.

Or, learn more https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVmliZSBDaGVjazogR1BULTUuNiBTb2wgSXMgT3VyIEZhdm9yaXRlIE1vZGVsIHRvIENvbGxhYm9yYXRlIFdpdGgiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzM0LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwcm9tbyIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjZ9.

newsletters.title: Want to join us in Brooklyn?
newsletters.content:
​

( https://0ab9ee3d.click.kit-mail3.com/xmu6zrxe6wf6hp520zxt5h2eo56r3unh8lr4l/kkhmh6hn0vlwwvclh7/aHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50bw== )

Our July IRL meetup is for paid Every subscribers.
Hi there,

We're bringing the Every community together outside for another summer evening in the backyard of our Brooklyn brownstone.

It'll be a casual hang with readers, writers, founders, operators, and creative people thinking seriously about AI, work, ideas, and what they're building next.

Every IRL—July 2026
Wednesday, July 15
6–8 p.m. ET
Brooklyn, NY

This event is for paid subscribers. Upgrade to request a spot.

Subscribe and RSVP ( https://0ab9ee3d.click.kit-mail3.com/xmu6zrxe6wf6hp520zxt5h2eo56r3unh8lr4l/58hvh7hgd2mxx8u6h4/aHR0cHM6Ly9sdW1hLmNvbS9oc2t6ejJiMQ== )

Hope to see you outside,
The Every team
